title: Venetian Serenade.
content:
Come, come to me, love!
        Come, love!--Arise
    And shame the bright stars
        With the light of thine eyes;
    Look out from thy lattice--
        Oh, lady-bird, hear!
    A swan on the water--
        My gondola's near!

    Come, come to me, love!
        Come, love!--My bride!
    O'er crystal in moonbeams
        We'll tranquilly glide:
    In the dip of the oar
        A melody flows
    Sweet as the nightingale
        Sings to the rose.

    Come, come to me, love!
        Come, love!--The day
    Brings warder and cloister!
        Away, then--away!
    Oh, haste to thy lover!
        Not yon star above
    Is more true to heaven
        Then he to his love!
meaning:
"Venetian Serenade." is a quintessential example of George Pope Morris's signature style...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
